subject: Social Science (Civics)
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the concept of law in promoting social justice
Understand how laws protect workers from exploitation
Evaluate the importance of minimum wage laws
Examine issues related to child labour and labour rights
Understand the role of safety laws and environmental regulations
Develop critical awareness about government responsibility in enforcing laws

lesson_plan.title: Law and Social Justice in Society
topics_covered: Importance of laws in protecting weaker sections
student_activities: Students analyze situations where laws protect people
Discuss why laws are necessary for fairness in society

lesson_plan.title: Workers’ Rights and Labour Laws
topics_covered: Role of labour laws in protecting workers
student_activities: Identify problems faced by workers in industries
Discuss how labour laws improve working conditions

lesson_plan.title: Minimum Wage and Fair Payment
student_activities: Analyze why workers need minimum wage protection
Write how minimum wage laws prevent exploitation

lesson_plan.title: Child Labour and Legal Protection
topics_covered: Causes and consequences of child labour
student_activities: Discuss real-life situations of child labour

lesson_plan.title: Safety Laws and Environmental Responsibility
student_activities: Analyze an example of an industrial accident
Discuss why safety and environmental laws are necessary

lesson_plan.title: Application and Chapter Review
topics_covered: Role of government in enforcing laws
student_activities: Write a paragraph on how laws promote social justice

teaching_aids: Charts on labour laws and safety regulations
teacher_tip: Use real-life case studies of labour rights, workplace safety, and environmental protection to help students understand how laws ensure social justice.
